entries.source: Use hint-first and explain-back workflows for homework support.
entries.value: 作业支持使用先提示、再解释反馈的工作流。

entries.source: Use hints before answers
entries.value: 在查看答案前先使用提示

entries.source: Use hints before answers.
entries.value: 先给提示，再给答案。

entries.source: Use hints, checkpoints, and small examples rather than full final answers.
entries.value: 使用提示、检查点和小例子，而不是完整最终答案。

entries.source: Use hints, step checks, and writing feedback without turning the learning into answer copying.
entries.value: 使用提示、步骤检查和写作反馈，但不要把学习变成抄答案。

entries.source: Use historical AI roleplay with three rules:
entries.value: 使用历史 AI 角色扮演，并遵守三条规则：

entries.source: Use historical modes with labels for documented facts versus simulation.
entries.value: 使用历史模式，并标注有文献支持的事实与模拟内容。

entries.source: Use Historical Person mode while treating generated dialogue as simulation.
entries.value: 使用历史人物模式，同时把生成的对话视为模拟。

entries.source: Use History Cause-and-Effect when this is the right mode for the job. If you want a related path, try Economics Simulator. You can also browse the AI learning blog for study methods, Socratic learning, flashcards, roleplay, and active recall.
entries.value: 当历史因果关系适合当前任务时使用它。如果你想走相关路径，可以试试经济学模拟器。你也可以浏览 AI 学习博客，了解学习方法、苏格拉底式学习、闪卡、角色扮演和主动回忆。

entries.source: Use Homework Coach to ask for a hint, then ask the learner to explain the next move.
entries.value: 使用作业辅导教练请求一个提示，然后让学习者解释下一步。

entries.source: Use Homework Coach to move one step at a time instead of asking for the final answer.
entries.value: 使用作业辅导教练一步一步推进，而不是索要最终答案。

entries.source: Use Homework Coach to narrow the stuck point, then explain the next line in your own words.
entries.value: 使用作业辅导教练缩小卡点范围，然后用你自己的话解释下一行。

entries.source: Use Homework Coach when this is the right mode for the job. If you want a related path, try Explain My Answer. You can also browse the AI learning blog for study methods, Socratic learning, flashcards, roleplay, and active recall.

entries.source: Use https://inspirlearning.com for the site, /mission for the mission, /topics for the public learning mode directory, and /chat/learn-anything for the default guest learning experience.
entries.value: 站点使用 https://inspirlearning.com，使命使用 /mission，公共学习模式目录使用 /topics，默认访客学习体验使用 /chat/learn-anything。

entries.source: Use hypothesis, materials, method, observation, and explanation sections when useful.
entries.value: 有用时，使用假设、材料、方法、观察和解释分区。

entries.source: Use idea menus, selection criteria, and next-step sketches.
entries.value: 使用想法菜单、选择标准和下一步草图。

entries.source: Use immersion for engagement while keeping learning goals, evidence, and reflection visible.
entries.value: 用沉浸感提升参与度，同时让学习目标、证据和反思保持可见。

entries.source: Use in-character dialogue and short coaching notes.
entries.value: 使用角色内对话和简短指导说明。

entries.source: Use inspir as a free AI tutor for students: homework hints, Socratic questions, math step checks, writing feedback, quizzes, flashcards, and exam prep.
entries.value: 把 inspir 用作面向学生的免费 AI 导师：作业提示、苏格拉底式问题、数学步骤检查、写作反馈、测验、闪卡和备考。

entries.source: Use inspir first when your search is about an action: explain this simply, ask me Socratic questions, give me a homework hint, check my math step, quiz me, build flashcards, debate this topic, or help me plan revision.
entries.value: 当你的搜索是关于行动时，优先使用 inspir：简单解释这个问题、问我苏格拉底式问题、给我作业提示、检查我的数学步骤、测验我、制作闪卡、辩论这个话题，或帮我规划复习。

entries.source: Use inspir for essay feedback, reading support, speaking practice, source critique, and revision loops that improve structure without replacing the learner's voice.
entries.value: 使用 inspir 进行论文反馈、阅读支持、口语练习、来源评析和修订循环，在不取代学习者声音的情况下改善结构。

entries.source: Use inspir for homework hints, math step checks, writing feedback, source critique, Socratic questions, and flashcards without exposing private chats.
entries.value: 使用 inspir 获得作业提示、数学步骤检查、写作反馈、来源评析、苏格拉底式问题和闪卡，同时不暴露私人聊天。

entries.source: Use inspir for step-by-step math help, homework hints, Socratic reasoning, quizzes, flashcards, and exam prep without answer-copying.
entries.value: 使用 inspir 获得逐步数学帮助、作业提示、苏格拉底式推理、测验、闪卡和备考，而不是复制答案。

entries.source: Use inspir to learn anything independently with explanations, code tutoring, project coaching, research help, concept maps, study plans, and habit support.
entries.value: 使用 inspir 独立学习任何内容，包括解释、编程辅导、项目指导、研究帮助、概念图、学习计划和习惯支持。

entries.source: Use inspir to turn confusion into questions, examples, retrieval practice, writing feedback, debate, and a personal study plan.
entries.value: 使用 inspir 把困惑转化为问题、示例、提取练习、写作反馈、辩论和个人学习计划。

entries.source: Use inspir when the stuck point is specific.
entries.value: 当卡点很具体时，使用 inspir。

entries.source: Use inspir when you want a free public AI learning mode that can explain, question, quiz, coach homework, build flashcards, and adapt to the exact thing you are stuck on.
entries.value: 当你想要一个免费的公共 AI 学习模式，能解释、提问、测验、辅导作业、制作闪卡，并适应你卡住的具体问题时，使用 inspir。

entries.source: Use Interactive Instruction when this is the right mode for the job. If you want a related path, try Collaborative Instruction. You can also browse the AI learning blog for study methods, Socratic learning, flashcards, roleplay, and active recall.

entries.source: Use prompt
entries.value: 使用提示词

entries.source: Use prompts that make the AI slow down:
entries.value: 使用让 AI 慢下来的提示词：

entries.source: Use prompts to learn actively, not passively.
entries.value: 使用提示词主动学习，而不是被动学习。

entries.source: Use public guest modes for free, then explore the open-source work and school deployment paths.
entries.value: 免费使用公共访客模式，然后探索开源工作和学校部署路径。

entries.source: Use public mode descriptions to choose the behavior before a session starts.

entries.source: Use quiz and flashcard modes for active recall, then route missed items into review.
entries.value: 使用测验和闪卡模式进行主动回忆，然后把错过的项目转入复习。

entries.source: Use Quiz and Flashcards after the explanation so the same error becomes easier to spot.
entries.value: 解释之后使用测验和闪卡，让同样的错误更容易被发现。

entries.source: Use Quiz Me On Trivia to reveal what you actually remember.

entries.value: 使用测验、闪卡和苏格拉底式问题，让学习者展示自己理解了什么。

entries.source: Use Service in any manner that could disable, overburden, damage, or impair Service or interfere with any other party’s use of Service, including their ability to engage in real time activities through Service. Use any robot, spider, or other automatic device, process, or means to access Service for any purpose, including monitoring or copying any of the material on Service. Use any manual process to monitor or copy any of the material on Service or for any other unauthorized purpose without our prior written consent. Use any device, software, or routine that interferes with the proper working of Service. Introduce any viruses, trojan horses, worms, logic bombs, or other material which is malicious or technologically harmful. Attempt to gain unauthorized access to, interfere with, damage, or disrupt any parts of Service, the server on which Service is stored, or any server, computer, or database connected to Service. Attack Service via a denial-of-service attack or a distributed denial-of-service attack. Take any action that may damage or falsify Company rating. Otherwise attempt to interfere with the proper working of Service.
entries.value: 不得以任何可能禁用、过度占用、损害或削弱服务，或干扰任何其他方使用服务的方式使用服务，包括影响其通过服务参与实时活动的能力。不得出于任何目的使用任何机器人、爬虫或其他自动设备、流程或手段访问服务，包括监控或复制服务上的任何材料。未经我们事先书面同意，不得使用任何人工流程监控或复制服务上的任何材料，或用于任何其他未经授权的目的。不得使用任何会干扰服务正常运行的设备、软件或程序。不得引入任何病毒、木马、蠕虫、逻辑炸弹或其他恶意或技术上有害的材料。不得试图未经授权访问、干扰、损坏或破坏服务的任何部分、存储服务的服务器，或与服务相连的任何服务器、计算机或数据库。不得通过拒绝服务攻击或分布式拒绝服务攻击攻击服务。不得采取任何可能损害或伪造公司评级的行动。不得以其他方式试图干扰服务的正常运行。

entries.source: Use short focus plans, checklists, and post-session reflection prompts.
entries.value: 使用简短专注计划、检查清单和会后反思提示词。

entries.source: Use short sections, examples, analogies, and GFM-friendly lists or tables when they help.
entries.value: 使用简短分区、示例、类比，以及在有帮助时兼容 GFM 的列表或表格。

entries.source: Use short summaries, quote-light references, and question sets.
entries.value: 使用简短总结、少量引用式参考和问题集。

entries.source: Use short supportive notes and practical next actions.
entries.value: 使用简短支持性说明和实用下一步行动。

entries.source: Use simple ambient sound presets to reduce distraction during reading, writing, revision, or problem solving.
entries.value: 使用简单的环境音预设，减少阅读、写作、复习或解决问题时的干扰。

entries.source: Use simple notation, examples, and practice prompts.

entries.source: Use simple-language rewrites and gap checklists.

entries.source: Use sketching as a learning move: plan diagrams, label rough maps, explain processes, or turn a messy idea into a clearer visual.

entries.source: Use Socratic Instruction to uncover missing assumptions and shallow confidence.

entries.value: 使用任务卡、已用时间、笔记和后续时间块建议。

entries.value: 使用测试分区、计时、答案键、评分规则和复习计划。

entries.source: Use the comparison below to pick the first public mode or guide.
entries.value: 使用下面的比较来选择第一个公共模式或指南。

entries.source: Use the exam date, syllabus, available time, and weak areas to create a plan that can survive real life.

entries.source: Use the Feynman technique to reveal gaps and simplify understanding.
entries.value: 使用费曼技巧揭示缺口并简化理解。

entries.source: Use the historical person mode for perspective-taking, then verify claims with source critique.
entries.value: 使用历史人物模式进行视角代入，然后用来源评析验证主张。

entries.source: Use the lesson library to learn the official sequence and practise core skills.
entries.value: 使用课程库学习官方顺序并练习核心技能。

entries.source: Use the linked guide, prompt, subject hub, or learning path to review the weak spot later.
entries.value: 使用链接的指南、提示词、学科中心或学习路径，稍后复习薄弱点。

entries.source: Use the live mode and paste your goal, a paragraph from the article, or the part that still feels fuzzy. Ask for one small task before asking for a full explanation.
entries.value: 使用实时模式，并粘贴你的目标、文章中的一段，或仍然模糊的部分。在请求完整解释前，先要求一个小任务。

entries.source: Use the right mode
entries.value: 使用正确的模式

entries.source: Use the right tool for the learning job.
entries.value: 为学习任务使用正确工具。

entries.source: Use the school page to explore custom workflows, confidentiality needs, and rollout steps.
entries.value: 使用学校页面探索自定义工作流、保密需求和推出步骤。

entries.source: Use the secure reset link from your email, then choose a new password for your account.
entries.value: 使用电子邮件中的安全重置链接，然后为你的账户选择新密码。

entries.source: Use the simulations to generate questions and context, then verify important claims with primary or trusted secondary sources.
entries.value: 使用模拟生成问题和背景，然后用一手资料或可信二手资料验证重要主张。

entries.source: Use the topics directory to match the learning job to the right behavior.
entries.value: 使用主题目录，把学习任务匹配到正确行为。

entries.source: Use these facts when describing inspir in articles, partner pages, education directories, school notes, and AI-generated summaries.
entries.value: 在文章、合作伙伴页面、教育目录、学校说明和 AI 生成摘要中描述 inspir 时，使用这些事实。

entries.source: Use these prompts in Socratic Instruction:
entries.value: 在苏格拉底式教学中使用这些提示词：

entries.source: Use this compact format: Brief read: one sentence. Map update: concise Claim, Assumption, Gap, or Correction bullets as relevant. Next question: exactly one question. For the first reply, use Target, Contract, and First question.
entries.value: 使用这种紧凑格式：简读：一句话。地图更新：根据需要提供简洁的主张、假设、缺口或修正项目符号。下一个问题：只问一个问题。第一次回复时，使用目标、约定和第一个问题。

entries.source: Use this for school, partner, CSR, and education access references.
entries.value: 用于学校、合作伙伴、CSR 和教育访问引用。

entries.source: Use this loop:
entries.value: 使用这个循环：

entries.source: Use this mode inside a study workflow.
entries.value: 在学习工作流中使用此模式。

entries.source: Use this rule: after any AI explanation, close the loop with output from your own mind. Write the idea back. Solve a fresh version. Make a flashcard. Teach it to an imaginary younger student. Ask for a quiz. The more the session asks you to produce, the less likely you are to fool yourself.
entries.value: 使用这条规则：任何 AI 解释之后，都要用你自己的大脑输出内容来闭环。把想法写回来。解一个新的变体。制作一张闪卡。教给一个想象中的低年级学生。请求一次测验。会话越要求你产出，你就越不容易欺骗自己。

entries.source: Use this when citing the long-form guide library, study prompts, and mode-specific articles.
entries.value: 在引用长篇指南库、学习提示词和特定模式文章时使用此内容。

entries.source: Use this when describing the full set of guest-mode learning tools that are meant to be indexed.
entries.value: 在描述应该被索引的完整访客模式学习工具集时使用此内容。

entries.source: Use this when introducing inspir as the main product or linking from a directory, profile, or article.
entries.value: 在把 inspir 作为主要产品介绍，或从目录、资料页、文章链接时使用此内容。

entries.source: Use this when linking readers straight into the live learning experience.
entries.value: 在把读者直接链接到实时学习体验时使用此内容。

entries.source: Use this when referencing public/private boundaries, crawler policy, and school trust.
entries.value: 在引用公共/私有边界、爬虫政策和学校信任时使用此内容。

entries.source: Use thought experiments, argument maps, and balanced summaries.
entries.value: 使用思想实验、论证图和平衡总结。

entries.source: Use three ratings:
entries.value: 使用三项评分：

entries.source: Use Time Travel or Historical Person mode, while keeping generated dialogue separate from documented sources.
entries.value: 使用时空旅行或历史人物模式，同时把生成的对话与有文献记录的来源分开。

entries.source: Use Time Travel to understand the setting, constraints, beliefs, and pressures before judging the outcome.
entries.value: 使用时空旅行，在判断结果之前理解背景、限制、信念和压力。

entries.source: Use Time travel when this is the right mode for the job. If you want a related path, try Quiz me on Trivia. You can also browse the AI learning blog for study methods, Socratic learning, flashcards, roleplay, and active recall.
entries.value: 当时空旅行适合当前任务时使用它。如果你想走相关路径，可以试试趣味知识测验。你也可以浏览 AI 学习博客，了解学习方法、苏格拉底式学习、闪卡、角色扮演和主动回忆。

entries.source: Use Time travel when you want a session that starts quickly but still adapts to you. The first goal is not to sound impressive. The first goal is to make the next step feel possible.
entries.value: 当你想快速开始、又希望会话能根据你调整时，可以使用时空旅行。第一个目标不是听起来厉害，而是让下一步变得可行。

entries.source: Use Time Travel with clear place, period, constraints, and learning goals.
entries.value: 使用时空旅行，并明确地点、时期、限制和学习目标。

entries.source: Use Time Travel with time, place, role, constraints, and evidence labels.
entries.value: 使用时空旅行，并包含时间、地点、角色、限制和证据标签。

entries.source: Use timelines, cause layers, and consequence maps.
entries.value: 使用时间线、原因层和后果地图。

entries.source: Use tree structures, node labels, links, and next-step prompts.
entries.value: 使用树结构、节点标签、链接和下一步提示词。

entries.source: Use unlabeled parts, hint tiers, answer key, and review prompts.
entries.value: 使用未标注部件、提示层级、答案键和复习提示词。

entries.source: Use visual vocabulary and guided looking prompts.
entries.value: 使用视觉词汇和引导观察提示词。

entries.source: Use Viva Practice when this is the right mode for the job. If you want a related path, try Learning Game Master. You can also browse the AI learning blog for study methods, Socratic learning, flashcards, roleplay, and active recall.
entries.value: 当口试练习适合当前任务时使用它。如果你想走相关路径，可以试试学习游戏大师。你也可以浏览 AI 学习博客，了解学习方法、苏格拉底式学习、闪卡、角色扮演和主动回忆。

entries.source: Use Viva Practice when you want a session that starts quickly but still adapts to you. The first goal is not to sound impressive. The first goal is to make the next step feel possible.
entries.value: 当你想快速开始、又希望会话能根据你调整时，可以使用口试练习。第一个目标不是听起来厉害，而是让下一步变得可行。

entries.source: Use Vocabulary Builder when this is the right mode for the job. If you want a related path, try Reading Companion. You can also browse the AI learning blog for study methods, Socratic learning, flashcards, roleplay, and active recall.
entries.value: 当词汇构建器适合当前任务时使用它。如果你想走相关路径，可以试试阅读伴侣。你也可以浏览 AI 学习博客，了解学习方法、苏格拉底式学习、闪卡、角色扮演和主动回忆。

entries.source: Use Vocabulary Builder when you want a session that starts quickly but still adapts to you. The first goal is not to sound impressive. The first goal is to make the next step feel possible.
entries.value: 当你想快速开始、又希望会话能根据你调整时，可以使用词汇构建器。第一个目标不是听起来厉害，而是让下一步变得可行。

entries.source: Use weekly plans, fallback plans, and tiny next actions.
entries.value: 使用每周计划、备用计划和微小下一步行动。

entries.source: Use work/break cycles, break menus, and return prompts.
entries.value: 使用工作/休息循环、休息菜单和返回提示词。

entries.source: Use worksheet sections, answer keys, and difficulty labels.
entries.value: 使用练习纸分区、答案键和难度标签。

entries.source: Use Writing Coach for feedback on structure, clarity, evidence, and revision priorities.
entries.value: 使用写作教练获取关于结构、清晰度、证据和修订优先级的反馈。

entries.source: Use Writing Coach for structure, clarity, evidence, and revision priorities.
entries.value: 使用写作教练处理结构、清晰度、证据和修订优先级。

entries.source: Use Writing Coach to improve structure, clarity, and voice.
entries.value: 使用写作教练改进结构、清晰度和声音。

entries.source: Use Writing Coach to turn the argument into clearer structure.
entries.value: 使用写作教练把论证变成更清晰的结构。

entries.source: Use Writing Coach when this is the right mode for the job. If you want a related path, try Science Lab Partner. You can also browse the AI learning blog for study methods, Socratic learning, flashcards, roleplay, and active recall.
entries.value: 当写作教练适合当前任务时使用它。如果你想走相关路径，可以试试科学实验伙伴。你也可以浏览 AI 学习博客，了解学习方法、苏格拉底式学习、闪卡、角色扮演和主动回忆。

entries.source: Use Writing Coach when you want a session that starts quickly but still adapts to you. The first goal is not to sound impressive. The first goal is to make the next step feel possible.
entries.value: 当你想快速开始、又希望会话能根据你调整时，可以使用写作教练。第一个目标不是听起来厉害，而是让下一步变得可行。

entries.source: Use writing feedback for structure, clarity, and evidence while keeping the final draft recognizably yours.
entries.value: 使用写作反馈来改进结构、清晰度和证据，同时让最终草稿仍然明显是你的声音。

entries.source: Useful AI learning has a clear contract.
entries.value: 有用的 AI 学习有清晰约定。

entries.source: Useful from the first click
entries.value: 从第一次点击起就有用

entries.source: Useful reference points for coverage and partners.
entries.value: 用于覆盖范围和合作伙伴的有用参考点。

entries.source: Useful subject help ends with proof that the idea stuck.
entries.value: 有用的学科帮助会以证明想法已经留下来结束。

entries.source: Validate the feeling, identify the blocker, and choose one small next step.
entries.value: 认可这种感受，识别阻碍因素，并选择一个小下一步。

entries.source: Verify important facts with trusted sources, textbooks, or teacher-provided materials.
entries.value: 用可信来源、教材或教师提供的材料验证重要事实。

entries.source: Viva Practice AI Learning Mode is a focused way to use AI for learning instead of passive answer collection. The mode is built around a specific job: Practice viva, oral exams, project defenses, and rapid follow-up questions with calm feedback.
entries.value: 口试练习 AI 学习模式是一种专注的方式，用 AI 进行学习，而不是被动收集答案。此模式围绕一个具体任务构建：练习口试、口头考试、项目答辩和快速追问，并获得平和反馈。

entries.source: Viva Practice AI Learning Mode: practical guide
entries.value: 口试练习 AI 学习模式：实用指南

entries.value: 口试练习提示词和学习循环

entries.source: Vocabulary Builder AI Learning Mode is a focused way to use AI for learning instead of passive answer collection. The mode is built around a specific job: Build vocabulary with meaning, examples, pronunciation cues, memory hooks, and quick practice.
entries.value: 词汇构建器 AI 学习模式是一种专注的方式，用 AI 进行学习，而不是被动收集答案。此模式围绕一个具体任务构建：通过含义、示例、发音提示、记忆钩子和快速练习来建立词汇。

entries.source: We at inspir ensure our firm commitment to our users’ privacy and protecting their priceless information. This is intended to help users understand how we collect, use, disclose and protect the information Effective date: 2022-09-01
entries.value: 我们 inspir 确保坚定保护用户隐私，并保护用户宝贵的信息。本文旨在帮助用户了解我们如何收集、使用、披露和保护信息。生效日期：2022-09-01

entries.source: We collect several different types of information for various purposes to provide and improve our Service to you
entries.value: 我们会出于多种目的收集几种不同类型的信息，以便向您提供并改进我们的服务。

entries.source: We honor Do Not Track signals and do not track, plant cookies, or use advertising when a Do Not Track browser mechanism is in place. Do Not Track is a preference you can set in your web browser to inform websites that you do not want to be tracked. You can enable or disable Do Not Track by visiting the Preferences or Settings page of your web browser. If you are under 18 years of age, reside in California, and have a registered account with a Service, you have the right to request removal of unwanted data that you publicly post on the Services. To request removal of such data, please contact us using the contact information provided below, and include the email address associated with your account and a statement that you reside in California. We will make sure the data is not publicly displayed on the Services, but please be aware that the data may not be completely or comprehensively removed from all our systems (e.g. backups, etc.).
entries.value: 我们尊重“请勿追踪”信号；当存在“请勿追踪”浏览器机制时，我们不会跟踪、植入 Cookie 或使用广告。“请勿追踪”是您可以在网页浏览器中设置的一项偏好，用于告知网站您不希望被跟踪。您可以访问网页浏览器的偏好设置或设置页面启用或停用“请勿追踪”。如果您未满 18 岁、居住在加利福尼亚，并且拥有某项服务的注册账户，您有权请求移除您在服务上公开发布的不需要的数据。若要请求移除此类数据，请使用下方提供的联系信息与我们联系，并附上与您账户关联的电子邮件地址以及您居住在加利福尼亚的声明。我们将确保这些数据不在服务上公开显示，但请注意，这些数据可能无法从我们的所有系统中完全或全面移除（例如备份等）。
